perturbation.original_text: that FEDERATED INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT COMPANY, a statutory trust duly organized under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Adviser"), does hereby nominate, constitute and appoint FEDERATED ADVISORY SERVICES COMPANY, a statutory trust duly organized under the laws of the State of Delaware ("FASC"), to act hereunder as the true and lawful agent and attorney-in-fact of the Adviser, acting on behalf of each of the funds or accounts for which Adviser acts as investment adviser or subadviser shown on Schedule 1 attached hereto and incorporated by reference herein (each such fund or account being hereinafter referred to as a "Fund" and collectively as the "Funds"), for the specific purpose of executing and delivering all such agreements, instruments, contracts, assignments, bond powers, stock powers, transfer instructions, receipts, waivers, consents and other documents, and performing all such acts, as Adviser, or FASC acting as agent for the Adviser pursuant to the Services Agreement dated as of January 1, 2004 between the Adviser and FASC (such agreement, as may be amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time is hereinafter referred to as the "Services Agreement"), may deem necessary or reasonably desirable, related to the acquisition, disposition and/or reinvestment of the funds and assets of a Fund in accordance with Adviser's supervision of the investment, sale and reinvestment of the funds and assets of each Fund pursuant to the authority granted to the Adviser as investment adviser or subadviser of each Fund under the Adviser's investment advisory or subadvisory contract for such Fund (such investment advisory or subadvisory contract, as may be amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time is hereinafter referred to as the "Investment Advisory Contract").
perturbation.changed_text: that FEDERATED INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT COMPANY, a statutory trust duly organized under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Adviser"), does hereby nominate, constitute and appoint FEDERATED ADVISORY SERVICES COMPANY, a statutory trust duly organized under the laws of the State of Delaware ("FASC"), to act hereunder as the true and lawful agent and attorney-in-fact of the Adviser, acting on behalf of each of the funds or accounts for which Adviser acts as investment adviser or subadviser shown on Schedule 1 attached hereto (each such fund or account being hereinafter referred to as a "Fund" and collectively as the "Funds"), for the specific purpose of executing and delivering all such agreements, instruments, contracts, assignments, bond powers, stock powers, transfer instructions, receipts, waivers, consents and other documents, and performing all such acts, as Adviser, or FASC acting as agent for the Adviser may deem necessary or reasonably desirable, related to the acquisition, disposition and/or reinvestment of the funds and assets of a Fund in accordance with Adviser's supervision of the investment, sale and reinvestment of the funds and assets of each Fund pursuant to the authority granted to the Adviser as investment adviser or subadviser of each Fund under the Adviser's investment advisory or subadvisory contract for such Fund (such investment advisory or subadvisory contract, as may be amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time is hereinafter referred to as the "Investment Advisory Contract").
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "incorporated by reference herein" was removed from the end of 'Schedule 1 attached hereto'. Schedule 1 lists the funds and accounts for which FASC is granted power of attorney. By removing the phrase 'incorporated by reference herein,' it creates ambiguity as to whether Schedule 1 is legally binding or merely an illustrative list, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This conflicts with later parts of the agreement that assume Schedule 1 definitively specifies the Funds.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Adviser hereby agrees that no person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity dealing with FASC shall be bound to inquire into FASC's power and authority hereunder and any such person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity shall be fully protected in relying on such power or authority unless such person, partnership, corporation or other legal entity has received prior written notice from the Adviser that this Limited Power of Attorney has been revoked.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that Schedule 1 is "incorporated by reference herein." This makes it a legally binding part of the agreement, which is essential because Schedule 1 lists the specific funds and accounts for which FASC has power of attorney. The changed text removes "incorporated by reference herein," which creates ambiguity about the legal status of Schedule 1. Because there is ambiguity as to whether Schedule 1 is legally binding, it is contradictory to the statement that third parties are not bound to inquire into FASC's power and authority. If Schedule 1 isn't binding, this opens up a possibility where a third party would have to inquire as to the extent of FASC's authority, which creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Limited Power of Attorney shall be revoked and terminated automatically upon the cancellation or termination of the Services Agreement or as to any Fund upon the cancellation or termination of the Adviser's Investment Advisory Contract for such Fund. Except as provided in the immediately preceding sentence, the powers and authorities herein granted may be revoked or terminated by the Adviser at any time provided that no such revocation or termination shall be effective until FASC has received actual notice of such revocation or termination in writing from the Adviser.
perturbation.changed_text: This Limited Power of Attorney shall be revoked and terminated automatically as to any Fund upon the cancellation or termination of the Adviser's Investment Advisory Contract for such Fund. Except as provided in the immediately preceding sentence, the powers and authorities herein granted may be revoked or terminated by the Adviser at any time provided that no such revocation or termination shall be effective until FASC has received actual notice of such revocation or termination in writing from the Adviser.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'upon the cancellation or termination of the Services Agreement' has been removed. This means the Limited Power of Attorney no longer automatically terminates upon cancellation of the Service Agreement, but *does* terminate upon cancellation of the Investment Advisory Contract. This contradicts the assumption in the previous sections of the document that the Limited Power of Attorney is inextricably tied to the Services Agreement, causing confusion about the conditions of termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: that FEDERATED INVESTMENT MANAGEMENT COMPANY, a statutory trust duly organized under the laws of the State of Delaware (the "Adviser"), does hereby nominate, constitute and appoint FEDERATED ADVISORY SERVICES COMPANY, a statutory trust duly organized under the laws of the State of Delaware ("FASC"), to act hereunder as the true and lawful agent and attorney-in-fact of the Adviser, acting on behalf of each of the funds or accounts for which Adviser acts as investment adviser or subadviser shown on Schedule 1 attached hereto and incorporated by reference herein (each such fund or account being hereinafter referred to as a "Fund" and collectively as the "Funds"), for the specific purpose of executing and delivering all such agreements, instruments, contracts, assignments, bond powers, stock powers, transfer instructions, receipts, waivers, consents and other documents, and performing all such acts, as Adviser, or FASC acting as agent for the Adviser pursuant to the Services Agreement dated as of January 1, 2004 between the Adviser and FASC (such agreement, as may be amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time is hereinafter referred to as the "Services Agreement"), may deem necessary or reasonably desirable, related to the acquisition, disposition and/or reinvestment of the funds and assets of a Fund in accordance with Adviser's supervision of the investment, sale and reinvestment of the funds and assets of each Fund pursuant to the authority granted to the Adviser as investment adviser or subadviser of each Fund under the Adviser's investment advisory or subadvisory contract for such Fund (such investment advisory or subadvisory contract, as may be amended, supplemented or otherwise modified from time to time is hereinafter referred to as the "Investment Advisory Contract").
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Limited Power of Attorney terminates automatically upon the cancellation of the Services Agreement *or* the Investment Advisory Contract. The changed text removes the reference to the Services Agreement, meaning the Limited Power of Attorney *no longer* automatically terminates if the Services Agreement is cancelled. The contradicted text states that FASC acts as the agent for the Adviser pursuant to the Services Agreement, indicating a tie between the two. Therefore, removing the Services Agreement as a trigger for automatic termination creates a contradiction, as the Limited Power of Attorney is now, under the changed text, still valid even if the Services Agreement is terminated, going against the assumption that it's intrinsically linked to the Services Agreement as described in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: This Limited Power of Attorney constitutes the entire agreement between the Adviser and FASC and may be changed only by a writing signed by both of them, except that the Adviser may at any time change the list of Funds to which this Limited Power of Attorney relates by executing and delivering to FASC a later dated version of Schedule 1.
perturbation.changed_text: This Limited Power of Attorney constitutes the entire agreement between the Adviser and FASC and may be changed only by a writing signed by both of them.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'except that the Adviser may at any time change the list of Funds to which this Limited Power of Attorney relates by executing and delivering to FASC a later dated version of Schedule 1' has been removed. Without this clause, the procedure for updating Schedule 1 (list of funds) is undefined. This contradicts the earlier sections and Schedule 1 itself which imply that the list of funds can be amended by the Adviser alone through a later version of Schedule 1. The current statement says changes require a writing signed by *both* parties, directly contradicting the expected procedure for updating the list of funds. Now, the change of funds require to be changed by writing signed by both parties.

perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Adviser's unilateral right to modify Schedule 1, which is the list of Funds. The original text explicitly states that the Limited Power of Attorney can only be changed by a writing signed by both parties, *except* for changes to the list of funds in Schedule 1. By removing the exception, the changed text now requires *both* parties to agree to updates of the funds, which contradicts the original intent and the implication that Schedule 1 can be revised solely by the Adviser. The Contradicted Text (Schedule 1) shows that the Adviser 'revised' it on March 1, 2020, implying unilateral action. Therefore, the removal of the exception creates a direct conflict.
